title: Sprints — Timebox Management System
scope:
Full-stack implementation of the Sprints feature as the first timebox kind in the TimeboxManager architecture. Covers the Go service layer (internal/timeboxsprints as sole writer), REST API endpoints under /api/v2/timeboxes/sprints, page registry + navigation wiring for /planning/sprints, the reusable <TimeboxManager> React component with sprint-specific date validation, Samantha SDK _timebox 3-level addressing substrate, and documentation finalisation.

The DB layer (migration 025: timebox_sprints table with EXCLUDE constraint on overlapping sprint ranges per workspace+org_node) was shipped in commit 26e861a and is complete. All API endpoints are built on the v2 pattern (/api/v2/ prefix, RFC 9457 error format, messages.* constants).
value: Gives product owners (padmin) a dedicated surface to create and manage sprint cycles for their teams. Sprint cycles are the core planning primitive for work-item scheduling — they unlock future features like sprint burndown charts, velocity tracking, and capacity planning. The TimeboxManager component is designed as a reusable primitive so that future timebox kinds (releases, iterations) require only a registry entry + migration rather than a new component.
implementation_plan: Step 1 (00513): Implement `internal/timeboxsprints` Go service — CRUD operations, org_node validation, adjacency enforcement (B.start = A.end + 1 day), non-overlap check, and bulk-create transaction.

Step 2 (00514): Expose `/api/v2/timeboxes/sprints` REST endpoints — GET list, GET by ID, POST create, PUT update, DELETE archive, POST bulk-create — with RFC 9457 error format and padmin permission gates.
Step 3 (00515): Write integration tests for service (adjacency, non-overlap, bulk-create rollback) and handler (all 5 endpoints, coverage >= 80% on service package).
Step 4 (00516): Add `pages` row in mmff_vector migration for `planning/sprints` route — Planning nav group, label 'Sprints', padmin visibility; wire secondary nav deep-link.
Step 5 (00517): Create `app/(app)/planning/sprints/page.tsx` — PageShell wrapper, role gate (padmin create/manage, user read-only TBD), mounts TimeboxManager.

Step 6 (00518): Implement `app/components/TimeboxManager.tsx` + `app/components/timebox/kinds.ts` — sprint list table, create/edit/delete forms with date adjacency validation, Badge status, notify.* feedback, catalog CSS.
Step 7 (00519): Register `_timebox` addressing substrate with addressables.Service — 3 levels: cross-kind list, sprint collection, named row. Verify lint:addressables passes.
Step 8 (00520): Update `docs/c_schema.md` (timebox_sprints row) and `docs/c_c_timebox_manager.md` (resolve all TBD items with implementation decisions).
areas_impacted: API: `internal/timeboxsprints` Go service + 5 REST handler functions under /api/v2/timeboxes/sprints; integration tests
SQL: timebox_sprints table (migration 025 complete); page registry row in mmff_vector (new migration required for 00516)
UI: `/planning/sprints` Next.js route, `<TimeboxManager>` component, `timebox/kinds.ts` registry, Badge status rendering
DEV: Samantha `_timebox` 3-level addressing, lint:addressables compliance, docs update
feature_list: internal/timeboxsprints Go service — sole writer for timebox_sprints table
Adjacency enforcement: B.start_date must equal A.end_date + 1 day
Non-overlap enforcement: DB EXCLUDE constraint on (workspace_id, org_node_id, daterange)
Bulk-create transaction: N sprints atomically or full rollback
REST endpoints: GET list, GET by ID, POST create, PUT update, DELETE archive (5 endpoints)
RFC 9457 error format with violations array for 422 responses
Page registry row: planning/sprints — Planning nav group, padmin visibility
Secondary nav deep-link: /planning/sprints resolves as a real URL segment
/planning/sprints frontend route with padmin/user role gate
Date picker adjacency constraint in create form
notify.* success/error feedback on all mutations
timebox/kinds.ts registry — adding a new kind requires only a registry row
Samantha _timebox addressing (3 levels: cross-kind / collection / named row)
Integration tests: adjacency, non-overlap, bulk-create rollback, all 5 endpoints
features_extended: TimeboxManager design doc (docs/c_c_timebox_manager.md) — written as design doc in 26e861a; finalised with implementation decisions in 00520
work-items sprint join (00458) — currently reads sprint_id FK; will resolve to timebox_sprints row via v2 API in a future follow-up

DB + Nav: Add `planning/sprints` page registry row and navigation wiring
Frontend: Create `/planning/sprints` route with role gate
Frontend: TimeboxManager component — sprints integration with date validation
Frontend: Register Samantha `_timebox` addressing substrate (3 levels)
Docs: Update `c_schema.md` and `c_c_timebox_manager.md` with finalized sprint implementation

acceptance_criteria.criterion: service.Create() rejects create if workspace+org_node has overlapping sprint (EXCLUDE fires)
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: service.Create() returns error when dates overlap

acceptance_criteria.criterion: service.BulkCreate() rolls back all sprints if any row fails
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: service.BulkCreate() transaction is atomic

acceptance_criteria.criterion: service.adjacencyCheck() returns error if B.start_date != A.end_date + 1 day
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: adjacency rule enforced at service layer

acceptance_criteria.criterion: service.List() returns non-archived sprints ordered by sprint_date_start ASC
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: list response is ordered and excludes archived rows

acceptance_criteria.criterion: GET /api/v2/timeboxes/sprints returns {sprints:[...], count:N} for caller's workspace

acceptance_criteria.criterion: POST /api/v2/timeboxes/sprints returns 422 with violations array on date overlap
acceptance_criteria.proven_by: RFC 9457 violations array present in error response

risks.risk: Timezone handling in sprint date boundaries — DATE columns in Postgres are timezone-naive; if the frontend sends ISO 8601 timestamps rather than bare dates, the adjacency check can fail by one day.
risks.mitigation: Enforce date-only (YYYY-MM-DD) in request validation at handler level; document in API contract.

risks.risk: Race condition on concurrent sprint creation — two padmin sessions creating sprints simultaneously could bypass the adjacency check before the EXCLUDE constraint fires.
risks.mitigation: DB EXCLUDE constraint is the authoritative guard; service-level adjacency check is advisory (UX feedback). Document that EXCLUDE is the source of truth.

risks.risk: User read-only access TBD — role gate currently uses padmin only; if product requires users to see sprints, a permission code and gate change is needed.
risks.mitigation: Scaffolded as TBD; document the TODO in 00517 description. Follow-up story when product requirement is confirmed.

risks.risk: Samantha _timebox addressing complexity — cross-kind address requires listing all timebox kinds; if kinds.ts registry grows, the cross-kind resolver must be kept in sync.
risks.mitigation: kinds.ts is the single registry; cross-kind resolver reads it at runtime.
